ddls:
CREATE TABLE "commodities" (
  "commodity_code" INTEGER /* Unique code for each commodity */,
  "commodity_description" TEXT /* Description of the commodity */,
  "commodity_type" TEXT /* Type of commodity (e.g., grain, livestock, horticultural) */,
  "commodity_class" TEXT /* Class of commodity (e.g., cereal, meat, fruit) */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit in which the commodity is measured (e.g., tons, kilograms) */,
  "seasonality" TEXT /* Typical growing or production season for the commodity */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the commodity information was last updated */,
  "commodity_status" TEXT /* Status of the commodity (e.g., active, discontinued) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("commodity_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "states" (
  "state_code" INTEGER /* Unique code for each state */,
  "state_name" TEXT /* Name of the state or region */,
  "region" TEXT /* Broader region to which the state belongs (e.g., Northeast, Southeast) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the state information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("state_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_data" (
  "market_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market data entry */,
  "commodity_code" INTEGER /* Code of the commodity being tracked */,
  "state_code" INTEGER /* Code of the state or region where the data was collected */,
  "gross_value" REAL /* Gross value of the commodity in dollars */,
  "gross_value_rse" TEXT /* Relative standard error for gross value */,
  "local_value" REAL /* Local value of the commodity in dollars */,
  "local_value_rse" TEXT /* Relative standard error for local value */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was collected */,
  "production_volume" REAL /* Volume of the commodity produced */,
  "price_per_unit" REAL /* Price of the commodity per unit of measure */,
  "demand_index" REAL /* Measure of market demand for the commodity */,
  "supply_index" REAL /* Measure of market supply for the commodity */,
  "price_elasticity" REAL /* Price elasticity of the commodity */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the market data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("market_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_state_code FOREIGN KEY ("state_code") REFERENCES states ("state_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_commodity_code FOREIGN KEY ("commodity_code") REFERENCES commodities ("commodity_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_year FOREIGN KEY ("year") REFERENCES years ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_name" TEXT /* Name of the year (e.g., 2023) */,
  "financial_year" TEXT /* Financial year corresponding to the calendar year (e.g., 2023-2024) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the year information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, policymaker) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team to which the user belongs */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the user information was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "market_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the market data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the market data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser used for access */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the access log entry was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_market_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_id") REFERENCES market_data ("market_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
